title: Deadly Russian Strikes Rock Kyiv on Eve of NATO Summit
summary: Ballistic missiles were fired into the capital of Ukraine as Russia launched its second major attack in less than a week. At least 12 people were killed, officials said.

title: Trump Asked FIFA to Review U.S. Player’s Suspension. Now He’s Eligible to Play.
summary: The reversal of Folarin Balogun’s World Cup suspension is highly unusual. It was the first time since 1962 that FIFA has nullified a suspension for a red card received during the World Cup.

title: Andy Burnham, the UK’s Likely Next Prime Minister, Made His Name in Manchester. Here’s How.
summary: Andy Burnham was mayor of Greater Manchester for nine years. His time there came to define his identity — and his pitch to lead Britain.

title: How Manchester’s Bee Buses and Trams Helped Fuel Andy Burnham’s Rise
summary: Andy Burnham brought Greater Manchester’s public transit back under public control, making buses more frequent and capping fares.

title: Mallory McMorrow Suspends Senate Bid in Democrats’ Heated Michigan Primary
summary: The state senator sought to carve a middle path between a left-wing candidate and one backed by the party establishment, but she gained little traction.

title: Trump Administration Rolls Back Dozens of Gun Regulations
summary: Critics say the administration is weakening public safety. Proponents say regulations would be where they were before President Joseph R. Biden took office.

title: White House Criticizes Smithsonian Museum for ‘Extreme Political Activism’
summary: A scathing report released on the Fourth of July says the National Museum of American History downplays the role of the founders while emphasizing social justice.

title: How Trump Put Himself in the Middle of America’s 250th
summary: The president placed his image and initiatives front and center, blurring the line, critics said, between loyalty to nation and loyalty to him.

title: At Trump’s Direction, Federal Agencies Are Abandoning Discrimination Cases
summary: President Trump has tried to scale back anti-discrimination regulations that date back decades. Federal agencies have heeded his call.

title: China Test Fires Long-Range Ballistic Missile in the Pacific
summary: China’s firing of a submarine-launched missile came as Australia secured more defense deals with Pacific Island nations. Countries expressed concern about the test.

title: Wikipedia Is Battling for the Soul of the Internet
summary: The internet’s largest stockpile of free knowledge is under threat from MAGA, A.I. and foreign autocrats. A bibliophile ex-ambassador is here to help.

title: How Meta’s Threads Became as Popular as X
summary: The social platform that Meta once positioned as a rival to Elon Musk’s X now has 500 million monthly users. It increasingly resembles Reddit.

title: Searching for What Pretty Means After an Acid Attack
summary: Nafiah Ikram was steps from her home when a stranger threw acid in her face. Five years later, she is trying to rebuild her life and cope with a stunning twist in her case.

title: The Main Attraction at Africa’s Most Lucrative Horse Race? Black Wealth.
summary: In South Africa, where the Black majority was excluded from the economy for generations, the annual Durban July event is a chance to celebrate luxury and high society.

title: Bring Back the SAT, California
summary: Under the test-blind policy, more students are thrown into classes for which they are unprepared.

title: The Liberal American Jew’s Dilemma
summary: Over the years, I have moved toward the way of being Jewish that my father rejected.

title: Years of Warnings About Public Housing Preceded Venezuela’s Earthquakes
summary: Residents, construction experts and seismologists said for years that Venezuela’s public housing would be vulnerable in a natural disaster.
